title: navarretia parvula
intro: Navarretia parvula, also known as dwarf bluebells or dwarf trumpet, is a rare annual plant species native to California and Oregon in the United States. It belongs to the phlox family (Polemoniaceae) and is characterized by its small, bell-shaped flowers in shades of blue and white. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and reduce fever. The herb can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
